title: SEC, Africa Prudential, Coronation Registrars Promote Gender Inclusion in Capital Market - FinancialEDGE
description: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Africa Prudential Plc, and Coronation Registrars Limited have emphasized the importance of gender inclusion in the capital market. html:
The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Africa Prudential Plc, and Coronation Registrars Limited have emphasized the importance of gender inclusion in the capital market.

At the “She’s Included” Gender Inclusion Conference and Summit 2025, the organizations reiterated their commitment to promoting diversity and inclusion in the financial sector.

Speaking at the event, Executive Commissioner Corporate Services, SEC, Mrs. Samiya Usman, highlighted the need for more women to participate in the capital market.

Also speaking, Managing Director of Africa Prudential Plc, Dr. Catherine Nwosu, emphasized the importance of creating opportunities for women to thrive in the financial sector.

The conference brought together stakeholders from the financial industry to discuss strategies for promoting gender inclusion and diversity.

The event was attended by top executives from Africa Prudential Plc, Coronation Registrars Limited, and the SEC, including Mrs. Eselojor Veronica, Regional Manager North, Africa Prudential Plc; Chiamaka Ugo-Obidike, Chief Client Officer, Coronation Registrars Limited; and Mary Adeleye, Head, Client Services & Business Development, Coronation Registrars Limited.

 

LEFT TO RIGHT: Regional Manager North, Africa Prudential Plc., Mrs. Eselojor Veronica, Chief Client Officer Coronation Registrars Limited Chiamaka Ugo-Obidike, Executive Commissioner Corporate Services, Securities and Exchange Commission Mrs. Samiya Usman, MD Africa Prudential Plc Dr. Catherine Nwosu and  Head, Client Services & Business Development Coronation Registrars Mary Adeleye during the She’s Included Gender Inclusion Conference and Summit 2025
